opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My left eye has gone completely red and achy and the light is absolutely killing me — I can't keep it open in a bright room.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's got worse over the evening and I'm worried about my eyesight. I didn't want to wait until morning.

symptom_narrative.character: a deep dull ache in the eye, started gritty
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: ache is a 4 or 5, but the light sensitivity is unbearable
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: keeping the eye closed and lights off
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no discharge, no injury, no chemicals
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: maybe something similar once before but milder
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: hard to look at screens or be in bright rooms

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a stiff lower back in the mornings for a while

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: works outdoors, no recent eye trauma

objective_data_offered.value: the white of the left eye is red, more towards the coloured part of the eye; the right eye looks normal; no pus or discharge
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: phone-camera view; cannot assess corneal clarity, cells/flare, or intraocular pressure
